commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix face splitting — snap line to plane + use triangle lookup

Three fixes for face splitting:
1. Snap line endpoints onto face plane before 2D projection (fixes
   floating-point errors from matrixWorld.invert() causing missed
   intersections)
2. Use triangleToHEFace for O(1) face lookup instead of findFaceAtPoint
   (avoids point-in-polygon edge cases at vertices/edges)
3. Include updatedAt in edge geometry key so edges re-render after splits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(model-3d): HEMesh as single source of truth — edge rendering + kernel extrude

Replace EdgesGeometry (which shows triangulation diagonals) with topology-aware
edge rendering from HEMesh. Add kernel extrudeFace operation for SketchUp-style
push-pull directly on the half-edge mesh, bypassing the Manifold backend.

- Add buildHEEdgeGeometry() to render only real topology edges
- Add extrudeFace() with 3 cases: face-on-solid, standalone, split face
- Wire push-pull to use kernel extrude when HEMesh is available
- Add heFaceId to FaceData for O(1) face lookup
- Update snap candidate generator to use HEMesh edges
- 62 kernel tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(models): add GPT-5.3 and GPT-5.4 model support

- modules/llm/model_capabilities.py:
  - Add GPT-5.4 (1.05M context) and GPT-5.4-pro (no structured outputs) registry entries.
  - Add GPT-5.3-chat (standard, 128K), GPT-5.3-codex (reasoning, 400K), and GPT-5.3 fallback.
  - Update docstring to mention GPT-5.3/5.4.

- modules/operations/cost_analysis.py:
  - Add pricing for gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-pro, gpt-5.3-chat-latest, gpt-5.3-codex.
  - Update pricing date to March 2026.

- tests/test_model_capabilities.py:
  - Add capability tests for GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4-pro, GPT-5.3-chat, GPT-5.3-codex.
  - Add provider detection assertions for all new model IDs.

- tests/test_cost_analysis.py:
  - Add cost calculation tests for all four new model pricing entries.

- README.md:
  - Add GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.3 to provider listing, model table, and reasoning model list.

- RELEASE_NOTES_v3.0.md:
  - Add GPT-5.3 and GPT-5.4 to provider listing, capability guarding note, and model table.

Why / rationale: OpenAI released GPT-5.3 (chat and codex) and GPT-5.4 (base and
pro) in early March 2026. This adds first-class support across the capability
registry, pricing table, documentation, and tests.

affected:
- modules/llm/model_capabilities.py
- modules/operations/cost_analysis.py
- tests/test_model_capabilities.py
- tests/test_cost_analysis.py
- README.md

commit.message:
fix: Prevent duplicate Slack notifications with date-independent dedup keys

Root cause: The Slack dedup key included the posted date (company|category|title|date).
When the same job was re-scraped with a different date (e.g., search engine returns it
with today's date instead of the original), the key changed and the job was re-posted
as "new".

Fix:
- Remove date from primary dedup key → company|category|title only
- Add first_seen tracking per dateless key with ISO timestamp
- 30-day staleness window: genuinely re-opened roles can be re-posted after 30 days
- Backward-compatible migration of old date-keyed entries in slack_posted.json
- Legacy key fallback for entries already in the tracking file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix round 12 audit findings: memory leak, cycle detection, causality enforcement, authorization

- Fix ResponseHistory memory leak in decision tree (copy to fresh slice)
- Add cycle detection in delegation chain walkChain
- Release RLock before recursive walkChain to prevent deadlock
- Add Causes field to AddEdge for explicit causal linking
- Count only successful mutations in tick engine
- Fix false quiescence when errors occur during tick
- Replace panic with error in VisitAddEvent invariant violation
- Defensive copy in Registry.UpdateState
- Store idempotency now verifies hash matches on duplicate ID
- Sever validates edge existence and actor authorization
- Respect zero-value config in NewDefaultTrustModelWithConfig

  - 現在：訊息 ≥5 字 + 匹配關鍵字即派工（覆蓋率大幅提升）

commit.message:
fix: resolve sync progress not updating to 100% for multiple viewers

When multiple browser tabs view the same sync progress simultaneously,
only the first one would show 100% completion. Others would stuck at 99%.

Root cause: syncProgressMap memory cache might not be available for all
viewers, and the fallback logic only checked running tasks, not completed
ones.

Solution: Add fallback to query the latest completed task from database
when memory cache is not available, and restore it to memory cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pomodoro): shared AudioContext, configurable durations, session persistence, long break (#31)

- Shared AudioContext: single instance created at module init, suspended
  context is resumed before each beep; eliminates browser context limit
- Duration presets: 25/5 classic, 15/5 sprint, 50/10 deep work, and a
  fully custom option with work/short-break/long-break minute inputs
- localStorage persistence: mode, timeLeft, session, workDoneInCycle
  saved on every tick and restored on load (discarded after 24 h)

  (duration per preset); workDoneInCycle resets after each long break

Closes #30

fix(relay): use prompt_template_hash for program lookup in receipt commitments (#226) (#228)

spawn_inference was passing contract.purpose_code (e.g. "MEDIATION") where
a SHA-256 hex hash was expected. load_prompt_program validates 64 hex chars,
so this always failed silently, leaving prompt_template_hash and
assembled_prompt_hash missing from receipt v2 commitments.

commit.message:
Task 30: Ruby server helper — Rack middleware (#32)

* Task 30: feat: Ruby server helper — Rack middleware

Implement BuildBanner::Middleware as a Rack middleware class with:
- Git info extraction (SHA, branch, commit date, remote URL) via Open3.capture2
- Env var overrides for all standard fields
- BUILDBANNER_CUSTOM_* → custom.* fields (lowercased suffix)
- URL sanitization (SSH shorthand, userinfo stripping, .git removal)
- Token auth with short-token guard and production warning
- Extras lambda support with error handling
- Cache-Control: no-store, Content-Type: application/json headers
- server_started cached at init time
- Never raises — all entry points wrapped in rescue

34 RSpec tests covering all specified scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for Ruby middleware

- Move auth check outside data-building rescue to prevent auth bypass
- Clear sha_full when env SHA is shorter than 40 chars to avoid mismatch
- Add PROTECTED_KEYS denylist so extras cannot overwrite core fields
- Initialize all ivars to safe defaults before risky code in initialize
- Refactor _read_env_config to eliminate duplicate hash; log warning on
  invalid BUILDBANNER_PORT
- Use array literal for git log command instead of %w with escaped space
- Replace endless range (header[7..]) with .sub for Ruby 2.7 compat
- Revert required_ruby_version back to >= 2.7
- Extract stub_git and build_app test helpers to reduce duplication
- Add test for extras not overwriting protected keys

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: alex-claude <alex@alexs-Mac-mini.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

remove sudo requirement for Docker operations via docker group auto-detection

shell.ts auto-skips sudo for docker commands when the user is in the docker
group (cached check via `dockerNeedsSudo()`). installDocker() now adds the
user to the docker group with usermod. Removed all explicit root checks from
command entry points. System operations (apt, systemd, ufw) still use sudo
internally. Updated all docs, README, and CLAUDE.md to drop sudo prefix.

commit.message:
fix: env validation warns instead of throwing — prevents startup crash on missing vars

validateServerRuntimeEnv() was throwing a hard Error in production when any
required env var was absent (including the new CRON_SECRET not yet in Vercel).
This crashed the entire instrumentation hook and returned 500 on every request.
Changed to console.error so the app starts normally and individual routes handle
their own missing-config errors at request time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement notifications + fix INP performance

- Replace Dapr-proxy notification endpoints with direct DB queries;
  lazy-sync task-based notification records (overdue, due-soon) on
  each poll so bell badge and dropdown work without Dapr sidecar
- Add refreshTrigger/onRead wiring so bell count updates immediately
  after marking notifications read
- Wrap filter/sort state updates in useTransition so filter changes
  don't block user input (main INP fix)
- Add React.memo to TaskItem (custom updated_at comparator),
  FilterBar, SortSelect, TagInput, DatePicker to cut re-renders
- Stabilize TaskForm child callbacks with useCallback so memo'd
  children can bail out on title/description keystrokes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve 12 vulnerability findings (rollup, minimatch, pg, express)

Remediation of all 12 open vulnerability findings for the CRANIS2 product:

Real fixes (3):
- rollup@4.57.1 → 4.59.0 (CVE-2026-27606, CRITICAL CVSS 9.8) — already
  resolved via Vite's transitive dependency in frontend
- minimatch@9.0.8 and @5.1.9 → 10.2.4 (CVE-2026-26996, HIGH CVSS 7.5)
  — added npm overrides in backend/package.json to force minimatch ^10.0.0
  across archiver's transitive dependencies (archiver-utils/glob and
  readdir-glob)

False positives dismissed (9):
- pg CVE-2017-16082 (CRITICAL) — affects pg@2.x only; installed pg@8.18.0
- 8 express CVEs (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) — all target Express 3.x/4.x; installed
  Express 5.2.1 has a different codebase and is not affected

All findings triaged via direct database updates. Zero open vulnerabilities
remaining; compliance gate now returns PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
